name: add-event-type
description: Add a new event type to the frontend feed system with corresponding React component. Use when user mentions "new event", "add event type", "event block", "new block type", or wants to display new agent output types.
capabilities: Define new TypeScript event type interfaces
Create React block components for event display
Update EventType union with new variants
Integrate with existing feed rendering system
author: AppleLamps
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation with TypeScript code examples for adding event types to a React frontend. No executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or cryptographic operations exist. The static findings in the prompt are false positives from the scanner misinterpreting TypeScript syntax and markdown code block formatting.
valueStatement: Users need to display new types of agent output in the chat feed. This skill provides structured instructions for adding new event types with TypeScript interfaces, React components, and WebSocket integration.
userTitle: Add new event types to feed
limitations: Does not modify backend event systems
Does not provide custom styling beyond Tailwind

useCases.title: Display agent output types
useCases.description: Add new block types to show different agent responses like search results, errors, or progress updates in the chat interface

useCases.title: Extend feed functionality
useCases.description: Create custom event displays for specialized agent capabilities beyond standard text and planning blocks

useCases.title: Add visualization components
useCases.description: Build new React components to visualize complex data like charts, tables, or formatted code in the feed

promptTemplates.title: Basic event type
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a new 'search_result' event type to display search results in the feed

promptTemplates.title: Error block
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an 'error' event type block that shows error messages with warning styling

promptTemplates.title: Progress updates
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a 'progress' event type with percentage and status text for tracking task progress

promptTemplates.title: Custom data display
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a 'chart' event type that accepts data points and renders a visualization in the feed

bestPractices: Follow existing naming conventions for consistency
Test new event types with mock WebSocket events
antiPatterns: Using inline styles instead of Tailwind classes
Forgetting to update the event handler switch case

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for adding event types to frontend applications.

faq.question: What event types can I add?
faq.answer: Any event type with defined TypeScript interfaces and React components for rendering in the feed.

faq.question: Do I need a React project?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill assumes an existing React/TypeScript project with a feed component system.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This skill only generates code patterns. It does not access, store, or transmit any user data.

faq.question: Why is my new event not displaying?
faq.answer: Check that you added the type to the EventType union and added a case in the feed rendering switch statement.

faq.question: How is this different from custom code?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured patterns ensuring consistency with existing code conventions and TypeScript safety.
